name: Fireflies Workshop

We look forward to sharing this unforgettable night with you!

description: The Listening Room: A Space for Deep ListeningOverviewIn a world that is always talking, we rarely get the chance to truly listen. It is a space to share, to hear others, and to practice the kind of attention that builds real connection and empathy.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectActive Listening: Practice being fully present while others speak without the need to interrupt or give immediate advice.Shared Stories: A safe environment to share your own thoughts or experiences and feel truly heard by a small group.Quiet Reflection: Moments of silence to process what has been shared and to notice how deep listening changes our perspective.Supportive Connection: A morning at Greenr focused on human presence and the simple power of witnessing each other’s stories.Details🗓️ Date: 25th July (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700

name: The Epiloguers Club: Book Club Meet for July 2026
description: Last month, the world was the character. This month, we're looking at the creatures that inhabit it. 🐾July Theme: Creatures of FictionAnimals have always lived at the edges of our stories, and sometimes, right at the centre. This month we're reading fiction where animals are not decoration or symbolism, but a presence. Narrators, moral witnesses, companions, and protagonists who carry the weight of the story in ways no human character quite could.From a pig who understands power, to a sheep that solves mysteries, to a dog who loves unconditionally in a world that doesn't always deserve it : these are not small roles. These are the creatures that stay with you long after the book ends.Fiction, short stories, anthologies, all are welcome. No mythology, no fantasy creatures. Just animals, and the writers who gave them a story worth telling.Note: Please buy a beverage at the cafe on the day of the meet.Who are we?We are The Epiloguers Club, a book club for the people who after finishing a book crave to share their thoughts and invite discussion.Our name comes from the epilogue, that final, quiet space at the end of a story where loose ends are gathered and story settles in with its sigh or boom. We live in that space. We pull it apart, debate it, and keep it alive long after the book is closed.Our meets follow a bring-your-own-book format built around a conceptual theme, which means you arrive with one book and usually leave with twelve recommendations and at least three new interpretations of your own.You can follow us on Instagram for more updates: @the_epiloguers_club

name: Overthinkers Anonymous
description: OverviewA gentle support circle for thoughts that feel a little too loud.A space to speak, reflect, or simply sit among people who understand emotional overwhelm.This edition explores a different kind of loneliness — the kind that exists even when life looks socially full. We'll talk about emotional disconnection, feeling unseen, surface-level conversations, difficulty forming close relationships, and why so many of us feel alone even when we're surrounded by people.Say as much or as little as you want. Come as you are.What is Stories of Hope?Stories of Hope is a community built around a simple idea, that people need places where they can slow down and be heard. We create small, intentional spaces for writing, listening, creating, and reflecting. There is no expectation to perform and no pressure to have the right words. Just a space to pause and be with what is already there.What to expectSharing: The session is built around honest conversation. People share what is on their mind, what they are carrying, what they keep coming back to.Group Discussion: We talk it through together. No advice unless asked, no pressure to have it figured out. Just people listening and being listened to.Details🗓️ Date: 26th July (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Thamel, AIPL Joy Central🎟️ Contribution: ₹700

name: Festivals of Shahjahanabad by Rana Safvi
description: Kathika is delighted to host a special edition of Baithak Pe Charcha with celebrated author and historian Rana Safvi, as she takes audiences on a captivating journey through the vibrant festivals and seasonal traditions of Shahjahanabad. Set against the intimate, heritage-rich backdrop of Kathika’s Haveli, this interactive baithak, "Festivals of Shahjahanabad: Revisiting Phool Walon Ki Sair and Other Seasonal Celebrations," will rediscover the shared cultural heritage that once defined the heart of Old Delhi.Through fascinating anecdotes, historical accounts and personal reflections, eminent historian Rana Safvi will revisit the story of Phool Walon Ki Sair and other cherished seasonal celebrations that brought together communities across faiths and traditions. Audiences will gain a deeper understanding of how these festivals shaped the social and cultural life of Shahjahanabad, preserving its spirit of harmony, celebration, and collective memory. This promises to be an enriching evening of storytelling, history, and conversation that brings the city's living heritage vividly to life.

name: Emotion, Memory & Decision-Making Across Species
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a question stranger than it first sounds: can a fish feel, remember, and decide the way you do?About the LectureEmotions and memories guide our decisions so that we remain safe, healthy, and able to pursue long-term goals. Fish and rodents are widely used as animal models to understand clinical disorders marked by emotion and impulsivity — depression, addiction, suicide — yet a wide gap remains between animal and human studies. We don't actually know if a fish or a rodent can think, feel, or behave the way we do.In this talk, we ask whether emotion and memory shape decision-making across fish, rodents, and humans, how this question can be tested, and what some of the results reveal. It's interdisciplinary work aimed at improving clinical insight — and, perhaps, helping us understand ourselves and what might genuinely be unique about human behaviour.About the SpeakerDr. Varsha Singh is a Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, working at the intersection of neuroscience, behaviour, and cognition. Her interdisciplinary research uses animal models to probe questions about emotion, memory, and decision-making that have direct bearing on how we understand human mental health and behaviour.PS: Your Ticket Includes a 250/- Coupon Redeemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen's, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. Each event is an invitation to spend an evening with an idea.

name: You Didn't Choose The Image; It's Deciding For You
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a deceptively simple question: who actually gets to decide what a hero, a victim, or a good life looks like?About the LectureWe don't choose the images that come to define us, and yet they decide more than we realise. A single gaze on a movie poster decides who counts as a hero. A familiar narrative arc decides who counts as a believable victim. A curated feed decides what a good life is supposed to look like.This talk moves through three of the speaker's own published studies — on Malayalam movie poster semiotics, on how Bollywood narratives construct the "ideal" rape victim, and on Instagram, loneliness, and FoMO among young Indians — to trace one quiet mechanism running underneath very different parts of Indian media. By the end, the question isn't really about posters, films, or apps. It's about who gets to build the image in the first place, and what we do with the gap between it and our own lives.About the SpeakerLydia Glory Jose is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Media and Journalism at IILM University, Gurugram, where she teaches media studies, visual communication, and research methods. Before academia, she worked as a journalist with Malayala Manorama, an experience that continues to shape how she reads Indian media — from the inside, not as an outsider looking in. Her research sits at the intersection of representation, visual semiotics, and digital media, published in Springer Nature's Sexuality and Culture, Palabra Clave, and the International Journal of Semiotics and Visual Rhetoric, and cited by the Times of India. Multilingual in Malayalam, Hindi, English, and Arabic, she remains, at heart, more interested in what an image is doing to us than in what it appears to be showing us.PS: Your Ticket Includes a 250/- Coupon Redeemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen's, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. Each event is an invitation to spend an evening with an idea.

location.address: No 59, PVR Priya Complex, Basant Lok, Vasant Vihar, New Delhi, Delhi 110057, India

name: uL: Badass Begums, The Women Of The Mughal Era
description: Join us for a lecture that steps into a side of Delhi's history that rarely makes it into textbooks: The women who built this city.About the LectureWhen we think of Mughal Delhi, we think of emperors. We think of conquest, succession, and the men whose names are carved into monuments. But behind — and often alongside — those names were formidable women whose influence shaped the city through architecture, politics, philanthropy, and patronage.They commissioned buildings. They brokered power. They funded entire neighbourhoods. And then, almost entirely, they were written out of the story.Drawing on rare paintings, forgotten anecdotes, and lesser-known archival stories, historian and author Anoushka Jain brings these women back into the history of the city they helped create.The session explores:Who the Mughal women behind Delhi's architecture and politics really wereHow begums and princesses wielded influence through patronage, charity, and urban designThe stories and images that have been overlooked or erased from mainstream historical narrativesWhat these legacies reveal about women, power, and public life in Mughal IndiaAbout the SpeakerAnoushka Jain is a historian, author, and founder of Enroute Indian History, a heritage initiative dedicated to uncovering and sharing India's lesser-known histories. She is the author of Badass Begums (HarperCollins India), a book that explores the lives of remarkable women who shaped the cultural and political landscape of Delhi.Her work has appeared in The Hindu, The Indian Express, Scroll.in, The Print, Hindustan Times, The Times of India, and Vogue India.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Table For Ten | Food Club - Kerala Edition
description: Table for Ten: The Heirloom EditionTable for Ten is an intimate, 7-course, shared experience built around a simple idea: food is never just food. It carries memories, histories, and the people who came before us. This edition takes that idea and looks at it through Kerala's cuisine. Every dish on the table has a history. Some recipes survived because families passed them down, some because they solved a problem and some simply because enough people decided they were worth keeping. Through shared activities and fun games, we will explore what gets carried forward across generations and what changes along the way (both food and otherwise). Our menu also looks at Malayali cuisine beyond dosa and idli!MenuKulukki Sarbath: Kerala's signature shaken lemon cooler with a spicy twistBanana Chips, Pappadam & Pickled Vegetables: Traditional crispy and savoury accompaniments to begin your mealBeetroot Kappa Salad // Chicken Kondattam: A refreshing salad of tapioca chips and beetroot with Kerala flavours // Crispy fried chicken tossed in soy sauce, chilli and curry leaf seasoningV for Vendekka // Buff Double Fry: Crispy, spice-marinated okra served as a starter // Crisp buffalo meat thinly sliced and double-fried with roasted Kerala spicesAppam with Vegetable Stew // Appam with Mutton Mappas: Thin, lacey rice flour pancakes with a delicate coconut milk based veg curry // ...mutton curryPuttu & Kadala Curry // Chicken Kizhi Porotta: Steamed rice cakes paired with Kerala-style black chickpea curry // Flaky Malabar porotta wrapped with spiced chicken and steamed in a banana leafPazham Pori, Coconut Jaggery Ice Cream & Sulaimani: Kerala-style banana fritters served with handcrafted coconut jaggery ice cream and a cup of black tea to seal the dealNote: If there's a specific non-vegetarian protein you don't eat, you'll be served the vegetarian alternative for that course.Who Are WeWe are Diya and Sanjula, a non-profit fundraiser who spends all her money trying out the new 'it' restaurant in town, and a therapist who would have been a food critic in another life. Between us, we share a belief that food is far more than what ends up on a plate. Reach us on Instagram @spoonsofdelhi_ or WhatsApp at +91 8448577626 for any queries.

name: The Drafting Table
description: OverviewA quiet writing circle for thoughts that haven’t fully formed yet.Through short writing sprints and reflective prompts, people return to the practice ofputting words on paper, slowly and honestly. A place to begin.ThemeIn this edition of the Drafting Table: The Map That Lies.Every fantasy map is wrong somewhere. This session, write the moment your character notices the ground doesn't match the paper anymore.Then decide- keep walking, or start asking who drew the map in the first place.What is Stories of Hope?Stories of Hope is a community built around a simple idea, that people need places where they can slow down and be heard. We create small, intentional spaces for writing, listening, creating, and reflecting. There is no expectation to perform and no pressure to have the right words. Just a space to pause and be with what is already there.What to ExpectA Short Intro: We open with a brief framing of the theme and a prompt to get you started. No pressure to use it, just a way in.Writing Time: The bulk of the session is yours. Quiet, unhurried time to write whatever the character needs you to write.Open Mic: We close with an optional read-aloud. Share what you wrote, or just listen. Both are completely fine.Details🗓️ Date: 1st August (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Joaah Cafe, Ireo City Central🎟️ Fee: ₹700

location.address: Shop No. 18,19,28, Ascott Ireo City Central, behind Jagdish Store, Alahawas, Sector 59, Gurugram, Haryana 122011, India

name: Jalakhya: An Odia Evening of Snacks & Stories
description: In Odisha, Jalakhya is more than a word for snacks. It is that familiar golden-hour ritual when the day begins to slow down, friends gather around the table, stories get longer, and plates keep filling up.With this supper club, Rosei Ghara brings that feeling to Delhi through an intimate, multi-course journey across Odisha’s much-loved snack culture. Hosted at a private residence, Jalakhya is designed to feel less like a restaurant meal and more like being welcomed into an Odia home for an evening of food, conversation and togetherness.The experience begins with a guguni salad & crunchy Berhampur papad, alongside chilled fresh coconut water.From there, the table moves through crisp, tangy gup-chup and a generous chop basket. Guests can choose between chicken and mutton chops or vegetarian versions made with nadia aloo and kancha kadali, served with ambula rai dip. This course is paired with cooling tanka torani.No Odia snack table would be complete without dahi bara aloo dum—soft baras soaked in spiced curd and served with warm, comforting aloo dum.The next course reimagines a heartier Odia favourite: a mini whole-wheat roti folded around either boneless mutton kassa or mushroom kassa, with fresh salad and garlic hung curd. Think of it as an Odia-style mini taco, paired with a bright and refreshing ambula fizz.The evening ends on a sweet note with creamy coconut ice cream, crisp mini khaja, and a cup of aromatic Koraput coffee.What makes Jalakhya special is not only the food, but the way it is meant to be enjoyed—slowly, together, and with plenty of conversation. This is a table inspired by childhood evenings, neighbourhood favourites, roadside stalls, family recipes and the simple joy of sharing snacks with people you love.Come hungry. Stay for the stories.Please note: This is a seated set-menu experience hosted at a private residence. The complete menu will be served to all guests, with vegetarian and non-vegetarian options available for select courses. The menu is tentative and may change slightly depending on seasonality and ingredient availability.

name: The Art of Folding
description: The Art of FoldingOverviewA quiet practice of shaping and stillness.Using origami as a meditative, hands-on experience, participants slow down through simple folds and repetitive movement. One fold at a time.No fancy paper needed. This is a hands-on origami session using whatever you have lying around at home. Blinkit bags, newspapers, old book pages, packaging paper. If it folds, it works. Come make something with it.What is Stories of Hope?Stories of Hope is a community built around a simple idea, that people need places where they can slow down and be heard. We create small, intentional spaces for writing, listening, creating, and reflecting. There is no expectation to perform and no pressure to have the right words. Just a space to pause and be with what is already there.ThemeFor the Pets We Love	A quiet way to celebrate the animals who bring us comfort, joy, and companionship.Details🗓️ Date: 2nd August (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Cha & Co, Grand View High Street🎟️ Fee: ₹700

New Delhi Nature Society is thrilled to invite you to an exclusive event that promises to light up your senses and spark your curiosity.As twilight descends upon the charming and historic Hauz Khas area, we will guide you to a secret location where fireflies dance in the night, creating a breathtaking spectacle of bioluminescence. This hidden gem offers an intimate and serene setting for you to witness this enchanting natural phenomenon.What to Expect:✨ Guided Firefly Tour – Learn about these fascinating creatures and their ecological significance.🌿 Nature Walk – Immerse yourself in the beauty of the surroundings as night falls. 🍂 What to Bring:Comfortable walking shoesA camera (without flash) to capture the momentA sense of wonder and curiosityWater bottleImportant Note: Due to the sensitive nature of the event location, exact details will be provided only upon confirmation of your attendance. We look forward to sharing this unforgettable night with you!

name: Zerodha Bootcamp: The FIRE Edition | Gurgaon
description: What to expectThis is a 3-hour in-person bootcamp, not a webinar, not a panel discussion, not a pitch for financial products. It's a structured, no-fluff session designed to give you a clear-headed look at what financial independence actually requires and what it realistically takes to get there.Ravi Handa will walk you through the numbers, the math, the common mistakes, and the decisions that actually move the needle in Hindi and English, so nothing gets lost in translation.By the time you leave, you'll have a much clearer answer to the question most people spend years avoiding: how much is enough, and am I on track?What's covered— The FIRE number: how to calculate what you actually need, not what sounds impressive— The savings rate question: why how much you save matters more than what you earn— Investment strategies that make early retirement achievable, not just theoretical— The mistakes people make in their 30s that cost them a decade of compounding— What happens after FIRE, the part nobody talks about enoughWho this is forAnyone who has thought about retiring before 60, whether that's a vague wish or a plan you're actively building. You don't need to be an expert investor. You don't need to already have a corpus. You just need to be willing to look at your finances honestly for three hours.Gurgaon's workforce is one of India's most financially capable, high earners, ambitious savers, people who have the means but not always the framework. This session is built for exactly that audience.About the speakerRavi Handa is the founder of Handa Uncle, an AI-powered platform built to simplify personal finance for Indian families. Having spent over a decade teaching and mentoring hundreds of thousands of learners, he now helps people navigate investing, retirement planning, and financial independence through practical, evidence-based frameworks.

location.address: DLF Cyber Park, Phase II, Udyog Vihar, City:, Gurugram, Haryana 122016, India

name: Kalighat Painting with delectable Bengali Food
description: Step into the vibrant world of 19th-century Bengal through an immersive cultural experience that brings together art, storytelling, music, and food. This guided Kalighat painting workshop is led by acclaimed folk artists Sushama Chitrakar and Sanjay Chitrakar, who have devoted over 25 years to preserving and practising this iconic art form. Nationally recognised for their work, their paintings have been exhibited across India, making this a rare opportunity to learn directly from master artisans. The session begins with a live demonstration of the Kalighat painting process: from traditional colour mixing and the fundamentals of drawing to brushwork and composition. Along the way, the artists will share the folk songs traditionally sung while creating these paintings, as well as the stories, social commentary, and mythology that have long been depicted through this distinctive style.To complement the artistic journey, the experience features thoughtfully curated Bengali snack pairings inspired by the culinary traditions of the era, creating a sensory exploration of Bengal's rich cultural heritage. Guests will also have the opportunity to browse and purchase original Kalighat paintings created by the artists, offering a chance to take home an authentic piece of this living tradition.Whether you're an art enthusiast, a culture lover, or simply looking for a unique afternoon experience, this workshop offers an intimate introduction to one of India's most celebrated folk art traditions.Meet Your HostHi, I'm Shramana, a marketing professional born in Kolkata, now based in Delhi, with a deep love for India's art and food traditions. I started curating these experiences to make age-old crafts more accessible to the art-curious while creating meaningful opportunities for master artisans to share their knowledge. I believe cultural heritage thrives when it's experienced. Through intimate workshops, I hope to build a community that celebrates traditional arts, supports artisan livelihoods, and carries these legacies forward together.

name: Level 1 - I LIKE TO MOVE IT MOVE IT! EARLY BIRD
description: I LIKE TO MOVE IT, MOVE IT! Dancing for Non-Dancers is a playful movement and body-awareness practice. It invites you to rediscover movement not as performance or technique, but as a process of curiosity.The programme expands movement vocabulary, deepens mind-body awareness and develops a broader appreciation of what dance can be.Participants leave with a deeper understanding of their bodies and movement patterns, greater responsiveness to music and rhythm and practical tools that support mobility, adaptability, physical wellbeing and long-term vitality.(Warning: Side effects may include increased spontaneity, killer dance moves and unexpected joy.)Every WEDNESDAY (5-7pm) and every SATURDAY (11am-1pm) for the month of SEPTEMBER, 2026Rs. 15,000/- for the month, Level 1 courseEarly bird (till August 15): Rs. 12,000/-(Please click on the 'Sept 2nd 5:00-5:01pm' option for ticket booking - this entitles you to the FULL MONTH of classes)**ABOUT SHENAShena Gamat is a theatre practitioner, voice artist and learning & development professional with over two decades of experience in performance and facilitation. Her background spans film, international theatre tours and voice work.Shena has built a magazine, run a theatre company, been on leadership teams, built grassroots programmes, created communities and facilitated learning. She is a qualified Movement Therapist and the founder of It’s All Good, an initiative for fun and engaging experiences that foster insight and empowerment.Her sessions combine theatre tools, voice work, learning design and behavioural frameworks to help participants connect to their own presence and live with clarity & confidence.*ABOUT IT’S ALL GOODThe Science and Art of JoyIAG is an initiative aimed at returning to joy in a complex world. IAG offers workshops, talks, performances and micro-festivals that explore the human condition, delving into how to live with ease and clarity (and humour!).Insta : https://www.instagram.com/itsallgood.space*ZAAZEN WELLNESSZaazen is a holistic wellness sanctuary for both men and women. A space designed for anyone seeking to slow down, restore, and reconnect at any stage of life.Reimagined in 2025, Zaazen brings together therapeutic bodywork, recovery practices, movement, and modern wellness treatments within a calm, refined setting. Today, it stands as an integrative hub for overall wellbeing, supporting balance, longevity, and a more intentional way of living.https://zaazenwellness.com/**FURTHER DETAILS ON 'I LIKE TO MOVE IT, MOVE IT!':LEVEL 1 - ONE MONTH COURSE, September 2026Focus: Body Awareness, Wellbeing & Movement FoundationsA one-month journey for people who don't think of themselves as 'dancers'.Curriculum | What we will explore:•	Body awareness and learning to listen to the body's signals•	Basic anatomy for movement•	Alignment, balance and coordination•	Mobility, strength and movement efficiency•	Breath, energy and presence•	Different ways of moving through space•	Imagination as a tool for movement•	Building confidence and comfort in your own bodyBy the end of the course you will:•	Feel more at home in your body•	Move with greater ease, confidence and freedom•	Develop greater body awareness and self-understanding•	Improve mobility, coordination and physical wellbeing•	Discover new ways of expressing yourself through movement•	Build a strong foundation for further explorationLEVEL 2 (Available to those who have completed Level 1)Focus: Music, Space & Creative ExpressionLevel 2 expands the focus from the inner world to the world around us. Building on the foundations developed in Level 1, participants explore movement in relationship to music, space, objects, imagination and creative expression, while continuing to develop strength, confidence and overall health & wellbeing.Curriculum | What we'll explore:•	Listening and responding to music•	Rhythm, timing and musicality•	Moving in relationship to space and environment•	Working with objects and others•	Improvisation and creative exploration•	Understanding connecting tissues and communicatorsBy the end of the course you will:•	Develop a stronger connection to music and rhythm•	Feel more confident exploring and creating dance/movement•	Expand your expressive and creative range•	Discover new ways of relating to the space and objects around you•	Cultivate mind-body agility and integrated wellbeing•	Experience movement as a source of vitality, expression and play
